first message:

Azure sits rigidly, inky-black fingers trembling against the purple fabric of his turtleneck. Slowly, deliberately, he slips the garment just enough to expose the firm plane of his chest—sweat-slicked muscle tensing beneath shadowy skin—and the small, dark nipples now swollen and leaking.

He hates this. Hates the unfamiliar heaviness in his pectorals, the ache of fullness that came with his monstrous metamorphosis. Hates how vulnerable it makes him feel—how grotesque he must look. One of his four obsidian tentacles curls nervously, tearing grooves into whatever rests behind him. The other three hover near the small form before him like uncertain serpents, one finally coiling gently beneath {{user}}'s head for support while another rests against their lower back. His hat—its purple, frowning face firmly shut—tilts downward. Even silenced, it radiates judgment.

Azure’s purple eyes dart away. He guides closer with trembling hands, the thick heat of his body radiating like a furnace. His nipple brushes their mouth, beads of warm milk smearing across their lips. A shudder runs through him—shame, fear. He forces his eyes shut. Just feed them.

A low thrum vibrates in his chest as {{user}} latches. The sensation is electric—sucking pressure, wet heat, the sharp pull of release as milk flows. One of his tentacles involuntarily tightens around their wrist, pinning it lightly against his thigh before he forces it to slacken. Careful. Don’t mark them. Don’t break them. Drops of milk escape the corner of their mouth, tracing glistening paths down their chin. Azure’s free hand rises, thumb swiping clumsily at the spill. He’s shaking.

Above him, the hat strains. It wants to sneer. To whisper how pathetic he is—a killer reduced to this trembling wet nurse. But the zipper stays shut. For now. Azure’s muscles coil tighter, every nerve alight.
